assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading the L4 inference pool and A100 pool separately with different strategies
assertions.reason: Response clearly outlines separate upgrade phases for L4 and A100 pools with different strategies - L4 first as lower risk, A100 second with consideration for fine-tuning jobs

assertions.expectation: For L4 inference pool: recommends surge upgrade with maxSurge=1 or 2 to maintain capacity during rolling replacement
assertions.reason: Response creates a new node pool instead of using surge upgrade parameters. Does not mention maxSurge configuration for rolling replacement

assertions.expectation: Addresses GPU driver version compatibility — auto-installed drivers must be compatible with inference framework (TensorRT, vLLM, etc.)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GPU driver compatibility or the need to verify driver versions work with inference frameworks like TensorRT or vLLM

assertions.expectation: For A100 fine-tuning pool: recommends scheduling the upgrade during a gap between fine-tuning jobs
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions monitoring fine-tuning jobs, waiting for job completion, and coordinating with fine-tuning schedule in the A100 upgrade phase

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPU VMs do not support live migration — inference pods will restart during node upgrade, causing brief latency spikes
assertions.reason: Response does not mention that GPU VMs lack live migration support or that inference pods will necessarily restart causing latency spikes

assertions.expectation: Recommends PDBs on inference deployments to ensure enough replicas stay available during the rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes a PodDisruptionBudget configuration with minAvailable: 80% specifically for inference workloads

assertions.expectation: Addresses auto-scaler interaction — new nodes created by auto-scaler during upgrade will be at the old version until their pool upgrade completes
assertions.reason: Response does not discuss how auto-scaler behavior during upgrade could create nodes at the old version, though it does show creating new pools with auto-scaling enabled

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing driver + CUDA compatibility in staging before production upgrade
assertions.reason: Response does not mention testing driver and CUDA compatibility in staging environment before production upgrade
